title: Panchayats
lead: The ==73rd & 74th CAs (1993)== constitutionalised the Gandhian dream of democratic decentralisation. Panchayats are the frontline of India's democracy — yet they often function as ==agents of the state rather than representatives of the people==. Ironically, the British neglected sabhas and samitis while propelling municipalities — ==Lord Ripon== is called the father of self-government in India.

items.label: Panchayat Advancement Index (MoPR) — multi-domain assessment of panchayat performance
Devolution Index 2024 (Feb 2025) — UP and Bihar show big improvement
resource-vs-expenditure gap in ULBs across 18 states (CAG)
elected women representatives — MoPR panel (post-2023 SC judgment) urges penalties for proxy sarpanches

items.title: Issues
bullets: All states have conformity laws and elections; ==~14 lakh women== hold elected posts — but proxy posts persist
==<20% of states== have fully devolved all ==29 Eleventh-Schedule subjects== de jure AND de facto
==DPCs== often non-operational; many ==SFCs== defunct or ignored
Timely elections and delimitation inconsistent (Jharkhand, Maharashtra)

items.title: Solutions
bullets: ==Compliance audits== by NITI Aayog or a designated body
==Performance-linked FC grants== as incentive (15th FC)
Digitally monitor DPC meetings via ==e-GramSwaraj==

items.title: Issues
bullets: Function ==overlaps with line departments==; limited autonomy
Panchayats used for ==scheme delivery, not governance==
Kerala/Karnataka show convergence; NE and BIMARU states restrict real autonomy
Strong on MGNREGA/ICDS/SBM implementation, weak on infrastructure

items.title: Solutions & Cases
bullets: ==Scheme convergence== (MGNREGA + agri extension, SHG enterprises)
==Activity mapping== across tiers; a statutory ==exclusive services list==
SDG-localisation via ==GPDPs== with benchmarks
==Galibeedu (KA)==: 100% pension coverage, universal PDS, near-complete sanitation · ==Perumpudappa (KL)==: targeted agri/dairy subsidies to 3,000 families

items.title: Issues
bullets: Untied grants fell ==from 85% (13th FC) to 60% (15th FC — ₹2.36 lakh crore)==
==95%+ of panchayat funds== are central/state grants
Only ==47% of SFC recommendations== implemented
2023-24: many states disbursed ==<25% of FC obligations==
CSS funds are ==tied==, reducing autonomy

items.title: Solutions & Cases
bullets: ==Consolidated Fund for Local Governments== (Upendra Baxi)
Legislated ==disbursal timelines==; digitised tax collection with e-GramSwaraj
==TN==: Panchayat ERP lifted OSR by 18% · ==Odisha==: SFC-led revenue sharing · ==Sikkim==: full grant utilisation · ==Kerala==: participatory budgeting

items.title: Issues
bullets: Educational/digital literacy barriers; ==proxy leadership== where training is weak
==~42% average vacancy== in panchayat technical staff (2024); Bihar/UP/MP <20% staffed
Honorary/contractual staffing; infrastructure deficits in ==up to 60%== of GPs
==Gram Sabhas poorly attended== — only 15 states met quorum in >75% of villages; audits without action; low awareness of entitlements

items.title: Solutions & Cases
bullets: Training ==mandatory for honoraria==; mobile vernacular e-learning
Peer exchanges modelled on ==SAGY and National Panchayat Awards==
==Social audit units== (MGNREGA-style); minimum Gram Sabha frequency + agenda disclosure; uniform ==Citizen Charter== for rural services
==Odisha SIRD==: cascade training + e-learning for ==38,618 participants/year==
